exercises.title: Phase-Flip Encoder
exercises.description: Implement the encoding circuit for the 3-qubit phase-flip code.
tasks: Create encoder using CNOT gates followed by Hadamards
Verify encoding of |0⟩ → |+++⟩ and |1⟩ → |---⟩
Test that |+⟩ encodes to equal superposition
Verify relationship to bit-flip code via Hadamard
Check orthogonality of logical basis states

exercises.title: Z Error Syndrome Decoder
exercises.description: Implement syndrome measurement and correction for Z errors.
tasks: Build X₁X₂ and X₂X₃ stabilizer operators
Implement syndrome extraction function
Test syndrome for Z errors on each qubit
Verify correction restores original state
Compare syndrome patterns with bit-flip code
exercises.expectedOutput: Complete Z-error correction cycle with tests
hints: Z on qubit i anti-commutes with X_i
Syndrome table is same as bit-flip code

exercises.title: X Error Vulnerability Analysis
exercises.description: Demonstrate and analyze the code's weakness to X errors.
tasks: Apply X errors and verify syndrome is (0,0)
Show that X₁ causes logical X error
Compare X_L operators for both 3-qubit codes
Calculate fidelity loss from X errors
Determine which states are most vulnerable
hints: Logical X_L = X₁ = X₂ = X₃ (any single X)

exercises.title: Code Duality Verification
exercises.description: Rigorously verify the duality between bit-flip and phase-flip codes.
tasks: Implement both codes in same framework
Show H⊗³ maps between logical bases
Verify stabilizer transformation under Hadamard
Show error correction circuits are Hadamard-conjugates
Prove general duality: HXH=Z, HZH=X extends to codes
exercises.expectedOutput: Complete duality proof with numerical verification
hints: Conjugating entire circuit gives dual circuit
Logical operators also transform under H
